enrichment.short_description: Digibunch is a free, open-source link-bundling tool from La Digitale that lets educators curate and share collections of resource links as a clean, shareable page.
enrichment.seo_title: Digibunch Review 2026: Free Link Bundling Tool for Educators
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Digibunch by La Digitale is a free, open-source tool for bundling curated links into a shareable page. See features, licensing, and use cases for educators.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary:
Digibunch is a free, open-source link-bundling application developed by La Digitale, a French educational technology project founded by developer Emmanuel Zimmert. La Digitale itself began development around March 2020 and launched publicly in November 2020, born from Zimmert's experience as a corporate trainer and the sudden demand for reliable, privacy-respecting remote-collaboration tools when the COVID-19 pandemic pushed teaching online. Rather than being a conventional venture-backed software company, La Digitale operates as a mission-driven open-source initiative, largely developed and maintained by Zimmert with community contributions, funded through voluntary crowdfunding and donations, and hosted on infrastructure from the Swiss provider Infomaniak, which the project emphasizes for its renewable-energy commitments. La Digitale is based in Cusset, France, with the broader project also noted as active in Copenhagen, Denmark, and its content is offered primarily in French, with English localization also available; the tool suite has become widely used across French-speaking schools in France, Belgium, Switzerland, and Quebec.

Digibunch's specific function, described in La Digitale's own materials by the French phrase 'offrir des bouquets de liens' (literally, 'offering bouquets of links'), is to let a teacher or content curator build an organized, visually clean collection of external web links to share with students, colleagues, or any audience. Rather than sending a messy list of raw URLs, a user adds each link individually and assigns it a title, a short description, and a color code, and Digibunch assembles these into a single, easy-to-navigate shareable webpage. This positions Digibunch as a lightweight, education-focused alternative to commercial link-in-bio or resource-curation tools like Linktree or Wakelet, purpose-built for pedagogical use cases rather than social-media profile links or influencer marketing. Documented use cases include building structured learning paths that combine several online resources in a defined order, compiling thematic bibliographies or sitographies to share with colleagues, organizing links and media gathered during a research project, and even constructing gamified 'treasure hunt' activities where each linked page contains a clue or riddle that leads to the next step, a popular format in French primary and secondary education.

Technically, Digibunch is built with a Vue.js-based frontend and a PHP backend (requiring PHP 8 or later), giving it a lighter, more traditional web-hosting footprint than some of La Digitale's other Node.js-based tools like Digiboard. It supports SQLite as its default database for simple deployments, with MariaDB or MySQL available as alternative database backends for larger or more demanding installations. The tool includes built-in support for privacy-respecting traffic analytics via Umami (an open-source, cookie-free alternative to Google Analytics) and includes domain-based security controls that restrict which external domains can be called through its API, a safeguard against abuse. Digibunch's interface and underlying translation strings support multiple languages beyond French and English, including Italian and Spanish, reflecting La Digitale's broader push to serve non-French-speaking educator communities. As with Digiboard and La Digitale's other applications, the full source code for Digibunch is published on Codeberg under the GNU Affero General Public License version 3 (AGPLv3), with a small number of bundled third-party assets, including the Roboto Slab typeface and Material Icons, carried under the separate Apache License 2.0, and the Mona Sans Expanded font carried under the SIL Open Font License.

Because Digibunch's code is fully open, schools, districts, or individual developers with PHP hosting capability can self-host their own instance rather than relying solely on La Digitale's own hosted demonstration environment at ladigitale.dev/digibunch, giving privacy-conscious institutions the option to keep all curated link data and any associated analytics entirely on infrastructure they control. This aligns with the AGPLv3 license's core requirement that any publicly deployed, modified version of the software must also make its source code available, which prevents a commercial vendor from taking Digibunch's code proprietary while offering it as a closed, paid hosted service.

Like essentially every tool in La Digitale's catalog, Digibunch has no pricing plans, premium tiers, or paid feature gates whatsoever; it is entirely free to use both on the officially hosted instance and via self-hosting. The project's sustainability model relies on voluntary donations from the educator community rather than subscription revenue, a deliberate philosophical choice that La Digitale positions in explicit contrast to commercial edtech products that often start free and later introduce paywalls or ad-supported tiers. Digibunch's core audience is K-12 and higher-education teachers, trainers, and school librarians looking for a simple, ad-free, no-login way to curate and share collections of educational resource links, though its general-purpose link-organizing function also makes it usable by any small team, nonprofit, or community group wanting a free, privacy-respecting alternative to commercial link-in-bio tools.

Digibunch sits alongside more than fifty sibling applications in the La Digitale catalog, spanning collaborative whiteboards, flashcards, mind maps, and presentation tools, all sharing the same underlying philosophy of simplicity, no forced account creation, and open licensing. Educators who adopt Digibunch for organizing resource links frequently pair it with other La Digitale tools for related classroom needs, and the consistent design language and privacy stance across the suite is part of what has made La Digitale a widely recommended, trusted source of digital tools within French educational technology circles, including endorsements and tutorials published by French national education platforms.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview

It lets teachers and curators organize collections of external web links, each with a title, description, and color code, into a single clean shareable webpage.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Digibunch is built with a Vue.js frontend and PHP backend, supporting SQLite by default with MariaDB or MySQL as alternatives, plus privacy-respecting Umami analytics.
The source code is published under the AGPLv3 license on Codeberg, letting schools self-host their own instance for full control over data.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Digibunch is entirely free with no subscription plans or paid feature gates, sustained through voluntary donations to La Digitale.
Its open-source AGPLv3 license also allows free self-hosting for institutions with PHP hosting capability.
